message:
fix(providers/gemini): reject empty Google Cloud project ID instead of using fallback

Previously build_google_cli_request() silently substituted "default-project"
when the project ID was empty, and decode_project_scoped_access_payload()
similarly fell back to "default-project" when the payload lacked a project_id
field. This caused confusing API errors downstream because "default-project"
is not a valid GCP project.

Now build_google_cli_request() returns Err with a clear message directing the
user to set GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT or re-authenticate with /login google-gemini-cli.
decode_project_scoped_access_payload() returns None when project_id is missing,
which propagates as an authentication error with an improved message that
includes the expected payload format.

The error handling in stream() is updated accordingly: the CLI request builder
result is mapped through Error::provider before JSON serialization.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
